answer:
To find \( P(\text{TP}) \), the prior probability of testing positive, we use the **law of total probability**:

\[
P(\text{TP}) = P(\text{TP} \mid D)P(D) + P(\text{TP} \mid \neg D)P(\neg D)
\]

Given:
- \( P(D) = 0.05 \), so \( P(\neg D) = 0.95 \)
- \( P(\text{TP} \mid D) = 0.99 \) (true positive rate)
- \( P(\text{TP} \mid \neg D) = 1 - 0.97 = 0.03 \) (false positive rate)

Plugging in the values:
\[
P(\text{TP}) = (0.99 \times 0.05) + (0.03 \times 0.95)
